svg.text: The brief pointed at a file that had been renamed. Did broken work ship?
never — it checked the brief against the code first
The one-line ask patched the wrong file every single time; the handoff found the real one and shipped working code.
A carefully hand-written brief recovers too — Foreman just writes that brief for you.
Stale details are normal in a living repo — the ask that checks first is the one that survives them. (smaller, cheaper model)
